text:
Once upon a time, there was a very nice man who fell into mud after stepping into a puddle. Afterward, he realized that puddles were bad company. Then, one day, he came home to find his cat curled up next to him on the couch. The cat smiled warmly. He thought it was hilarious when people tripped over their own feet. Laughing afterward at his own clumsiness was his favorite kind of amusement.

That night before bed, he envisioned himself tripping over nothing. Perhaps he'd catch his heel on a weed? Or maybe it'd be nothing at all—just him, the cozy darkness, and the sound of his own laughter echoing in his own mind.

The next morning, the cat appeared again. This time, he had a paw extended. The man looked down, expecting another roundhouse kick. Instead, the cat stared earnestly at him. "Want a goodnight kiss?"
